steps.dialogue: Welcome back! Let's continue your journey with fractions on number lines.

You've already seen that the denominator tells us how many equal intervals to make on a number line. You learned that fractions have specific positions, and you even explored what happens when fractions go beyond 1. Remember how [fraction numerator=4, denominator=3]four thirds[/fraction] was one more third past the whole? Keep that in mind because it's an important idea as we go forward together.

Today we're taking that understanding and turning it into precision. We'll master the exact method for placing fractions accurately - making exact marks, labeling correctly, and checking that every interval is perfectly equal. You'll discover patterns that make placement automatic!
You've put in a lot of work so far and have got the basics down. Today let's develop your skill at precise placement.
